black_powder_vaulter.name: Black Powder Vaulter
description: ## Black Powder Vaulter
‹SOURCE Martial Arts Handbook/10›

While firearms were designed to allow combatants to fight their foes from a distance, some gunslingers like to get into the thick of the battle, maneuvering among the fray to find the most advantageous positions from which to kill their enemies. The daredevil fighting style of the black powder vaulter has arisen simultaneously in two places - among Mana Wastes gunslingers inspired by the graceful fighting styles of Mwangi tribes, and among pirates from Tian Xia and the Shackles who encountered and inspired one another.
::ab[Deeds]{head icon=info note="A black powder vaulter gains the following deeds."}

::ab[Mobile Reload (Ex)]{next icon=power ability="If a black powder vaulter has at least 1 grit point, she can reload a firearm as part of a @HLmove_action to move, provided that the action to reload that firearm with that type of ammunition would normally take only a move action or swift action." imp3="She can use this deed while also using the *shot on the run* deed." replace="‹deed/Deadeye› deed"}

::ab[Daring Vault (Ex)]{next l=1 icon=boost swift="A black powder vaulter can spend 1 grit point to increase her land speed by 20 feet (gaining the appropriate bonus on ‹skill/Acrobatics› checks to jump) until the end of her turn. If the black powder vaulter jumps while affected by this deed, she can make a second jump while midair if she is adjacent to a wall, column, or other solid, permanent structure. The second jump counts as having a running start even if the direction is different from the first jump." replace="‹deed/Gunslinger's dodge› deed"}

::ab[Shot on the Run (Ex)]{next l=3 icon=power ability="If the black powder vaulter has at least 1 grit point, she gains the benefits of the ‹feat/Shot on the Run› feat. She gains this benefit only when making an attack with a firearm with which she is proficient." imp7="She can use the ‹deed/targeting› deed as part of the same @HLfull-round_action as using Shot on the Run." replace="‹deed/Gunslinger initiative› deed"}

::ab[Art of the Gun (Ex)]{next l=7 icon=def-melee passive="If the black powder vaulter has at least 1 grit point, she doesn't provoke attacks of opportunity when attacking with a firearm or reloading a firearm." order=pa ability="Additionally, whenever she is wielding a firearm, she counts as threatening an area around her as though she were wielding a melee weapon. If an opponent provokes an attack of opportunity from her, she can use the ‹deed/pistol-whip› deed as her attack of opportunity, spending 1 grit point as normal. Alternatively, if her firearm is loaded, she can spend 1 grit point to fire her gun as her attack of opportunity." replace="‹deed/Startling shot› deed"}

::ab[Dual Shot on the Run (Ex)]{next l=11 icon=ranged ability="The black powder vaulter can make two attacks with firearms while using the *shot on the run* deed." replace="‹deed/Expert loading› deed"}

blatherskite.name: Blatherskite
description: ## Blatherskite
‹SOURCE Antihero's Handbook/9›

A blatherskite talks big and feels powerful when holding a loaded gun, but when the gun jams or the situation gets too intense, he's the first to run for cover. He has an extraordinary talent for retreating from harm's way and fooling his enemies into thinking he is weaker than he is.
::cskill[Class Skills]{gain="Escape Artist~Stealth" lose="Handle Animal, Intimidate"}
::ab[Deeds]{head icon=power note="A blatherskite swaps four deeds for the following."}

::ab[Blatherskite's Stagger (Ex)]{next l=1 icon=power-down immediate="When a ranged attack would normally miss a blatherskite, he can allow it to hit him and dramatically stagger a few steps backward, moving up to 10 feet directly away from the attacker. This movement provokes attacks of opportunity as normal. The attack deals the minimum amount of damage (as though the attacker had rolled a natural 1 on each damage die)." imp2="If a ranged attack hits the blatherskite but the attack roll exceeds his AC by no more than his bonus from the *nimble* class feature, he can spend 1 grit point as an @HLimmediate_action to take the minimum amount of damage and move up to 10 feet directly away from the attacker. This movement provokes attacks of opportunity as normal." special="The gunslinger can perform this deed only while wearing medium or light armor and while carrying no more than a light load." replace="‹deed/Gunslinger's dodge› deed"}

::ab[Blatherskite's Initiative (Ex)]{next l=3 icon=boost-power ability="The blatherskite gains benefits as long as he has at least 1 grit point. The blatherskite gains a +2 bonus on initiative checks. Furthermore, if he is trained in Stealth (and as long as he is not paralyzed, prone, or otherwise immobilized), he can take a 5-foot step and attempt a Stealth check to hide as part of the initiative check, provided there is an object or a larger creature to hide behind; if he spends 1 grit point, he can move up to half his speed instead of taking only a 5-foot step as part of the initiative check." replace="‹deed/Gunslinger initiative› deed"}

::ab[Cheap Shot (Ex)]{next l=3 icon=ranged ability="If the blatherskite has at least 1 grit point and makes a successful attack against an unarmed target with a firearm that is not making a scattering shot, he deals 1d6 additional points of damage. This is ‹misc/precision damage› and is not multiplied on a critical hit." replace="‹deed/Pistol-whip› deed"}

::ab[Blatherskite's Surprise (Ex)]{next l=7 icon=down-lower passive="When the blatherskite succeeds at a saving throw against a spell or an effect that would cause him to be blinded, confused, dazed, fascinated, frightened, nauseated, paralyzed, or stunned, he can allow the effect to affect him normally. As long as he has at least 1 grit point, as an @HLimmediate_action he can cause the creature from which the effect originated to lose its Dexterity bonus to AC for a number of rounds equal to half the duration of the effect (in rounds) or ‹misc/half› the gunslinger's level, whichever is lower (minimum 1 round). If he instead spends 1 grit point, he does not need to allow the effect to affect him normally to use this deed." replace="‹deed/Startling shot› deed"}

bolt_ace.name: Bolt Ace
description: ## Bolt Ace
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/94›

While gunslingers are full of sound and fury, there is a class of gunslingers that never soil their hands with powder or feel the sting of gun smoke. They commit their deeds in a more hushed manner, but with just as much flair.

::ab[Crossbow Maven]{icon=down-power note="A bolt ace is proficient with all crossbows instead of all firearms and begins play with a masterwork ‹eq-weapon/crossbow› of her choice." alter="Weapon proficiencies" replace=Gunsmith}
::ab[Grit (Ex)]{icon=power passive="A bolt ace regains grit when she scores a critical hit or deals a killing blow with any kind of crossbow." alter=Grit}

::ab[Deeds]{head icon=power note="A bolt ace can perform the following deeds with a crossbow instead of a firearm: ‹deed/gunslinger initiative›, ‹deed/pistol-whip›, ‹deed/dead shot›, ‹deed/targeting›, ‹deed/bleeding wound›, ‹deed/death's shot›, and ‹deed/stunning shot›. The bolt ace swaps the following deeds."}

::ab[Sharp Shoot (Ex)]{next l=1 icon=lower ability="A bolt ace can resolve an attack against touch AC instead of normal AC when firing a crossbow at a target within its first range increment. Performing this deed costs 1 grit point. This deed's cost cannot be reduced by any ability or effect that reduces the amount of grit points a deed costs (such as ‹feat/Signature Deed›)." replace="‹deed/Deadeye›"}

::ab[Vigilant Loading (Ex)]{next l=1 icon=def passive="As long as a bolt ace has at least 1 grit point, she does not provoke attacks of opportunity when loading a crossbow." replace="‹deed/Quick clear›"}

::ab[Shooter's Resolve (Ex)]{next l=3 icon=lower ability="A bolt ace can spend 1 grit point when making a crossbow attack as a @HLstandard_action and ignore the effects of ‹rule/concealment› (though not total concealment) and ‹rule/cover› (other than total cover) against that shot." replace="‹deed/Utility shot›"}

::ab[Distracting Shot (Ex)]{next l=7 icon=lower ability="A bolt ace can spend 1 grit point and choose to miss a target that she could normally attack within her range with a crossbow attack. When she does, the target loses its Dexterity bonus to AC (if any) for 1 round." replace="‹deed/Startling shot›"}

::ab[Vigilant Shooter (Ex)]{next l=11 icon=def ability="As long as a bolt ace spends 1 grit point when she does so, she does not provoke attacks of opportunity when firing a crossbow." replace="‹deed/Expert loading›"}

::ab[Inexplicable Reload (Ex)]{next l=11 icon=power-boost ability="Loading a crossbow becomes unthinking and automatic for a bolt ace. As long as she has at least 1 grit point, she always starts each round of combat (even a surprise round) with her crossbow loaded. Also the amount of time needed to reload a crossbow decreases by one step: a standard action becomes a move action, a move action becomes a swift action, a swift action becomes a free action, and a free action becomes not an action." replace="‹deed/Lightning reload›"}

::ab[Pinning Shot (Ex)]{next l=15 icon=ranged ability="The bolt ace can spend 1 grit point while shooting a crossbow and attempt to pin down its target with the bolt. If the crossbow attack hits, it pins the target to a nearby object, to a wall, or to the ground, ‹rule/entangl«ing<ed› and ‹misc/stagger«ing› the target. While pinned by the bolt, the target cannot move out of its space until it takes a standard action to free itself from the pinning bolt, except by means of teleportation." replace="‹deed/Menacing shot›"}

::ab[Crossbow Training (Ex)]{l=5 icon=boost choice="A bolt ace can select one specific type of crossbow, such as ‹eq-weapon/hand crossbow› or ‹eq-weapon/heavy crossbow›. She gains a bonus on damage rolls equal to her Dexterity modifier with that crossbow. Furthermore, when she scores a critical hit with that type of crossbow, her critical modifier increases by 1 (a &times&2 becomes a &times&3, for example)." imp9="She can pick a different type of crossbow, gaining the bonus damage and increased critical multiplier with that type of crossbow as well." imp13="She can pick a third crossbow." imp17="She can pick a fourth crossbow." replace="Gun training"}

buccaneer.name: Buccaneer
description: ## Buccaneer
‹SOURCE Advanced Race Guide/73›

Freebooters who cling to the convoluted codes that rule independent ships, the buccaneer is a gunslinger of the high seas. Their exploits are fueled by the thrill of danger and often by the temporary courage provided by grog. A buccaneer has the following class features.
::prereq{r=Human}
::ab[Deeds]{head icon=power note="A buccaneer swaps four of the normal gunslinger deeds for the following deeds."}

::ab[Seadog's Gait (Ex)]{next l=1 icon=power ability="The buccaneer gains ‹feat/Sea Legs› as a bonus feat. If she spends 1 grit point, she can ignore ‹rule/difficult terrain› until the end of her turn." replace="‹deed/Quick clear› deed"}

::ab[Pirate's Jargon (Ex)]{next l=3 icon=boost-lower order=pw passive="The buccaneer's baffling palaver of nautical jargon and piratical cant provides a +2 bonus on Bluff and Intimidate checks." swift="She can spend 1 grit point to cause a single living creature within 30 feet to make a Will saving throw (DC = 10 + 1/2 her buccaneer level + her Charisma modifier) or become ‹misc/confused› for 1 round. This is a mind-affecting language-dependent effect." replace="‹deed/Pistol whip› deed"}

::ab[Rope Swing (Ex)]{next l=7 icon=boost-def order=pa passive="As long as the buccaneer has at least 1 grit point, she gains a bonus on Acrobatics and Climb checks equal to her gunslinger level when climbing or swinging on a rope." ability="If she spends 1 grit point, her move while climbing or swinging on a rope does not provoke attacks of opportunity." replace="‹deed/Dead shot› deed"}

::ab[Captain's Curse (Sp)]{next l=11 icon=magic ability="A buccaneer can spend 2 grit points to use ‹spell/old salt's curse› as a spell-like ability." imp15="She may use ‹spell/black mark› instead." special="The caster level for these spell-like abilities is equal to the buccaneer's class level, and the save DC is equal to 10 + her buccaneer level + her Charisma modifier." replace="‹deed/Lightning reload› deed"}

::ab[Grit (Ex)]{icon=info flavor="Like the sea itself, a buccaneer is a force of nature." note="Instead of using her Wisdom modifier to determine the number of grit points she gains at the start of each day, she uses her Charisma modifier. This ability works in all other ways like the gunslinger's *grit* class feature." alter=Grit}

::ab[Liquid Courage (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power-def order=ap ability="A buccaneer gains the ability to fortify her grit with strong drink. The act of drinking is a @HLstandard_action that provokes attacks of opportunity, and each drink provides 1 grog point. Grog points can be used in place of grit points to fuel deeds or grit feats (including those requiring a minimum of 1 grit point to use)." special="The buccaneer can gain a maximum number of grog points each day equal to her Constitution modifier (minimum 1), and they last for 1 hour or until used, whichever comes first." passive="She gains a morale bonus on saves against fear and a dodge bonus to AC against attacks of opportunity equal to her current grog point total." replace=Nimble}

::ab[Bonus Feat]{icon=info note="In addition to combat and grit feats, a buccaneer can select from the following feats as her bonus feats: ‹feat/Expert Driver› (water vehicles only), ‹feat/Master Siege Engineer›, ‹feat/Siege Engineer›, ‹feat/Siege Gunner›, ‹feat/Skilled Driver› (water vehicles only)."}

::ab[Exotic Pet (Ex)]{l=5 icon=power-def order=ap ability="A buccaneer gains a ‹sidekick/familiar› as a ‹class/wizard› of half the buccaneer's class level (though the exotic pet never gains the ability to *deliver touch spells* or *share spells*). This pet is typically a ‹monster/monkey› or parrot (use the stats for a ‹monster/raven› familiar). Such animals are useful scouts, even in the thick of combat." passive="As long as the pet is within 30 feet of the buccaneer, the buccaneer also gains the benefit of ‹ability/evasion›." replace="Gun training 1"}
::ab[Sword and Pistol]{l=9 icon=power ability="A buccaneer gains ‹feat/Sword and Pistol› as a bonus feat even if she does not meet the prerequisites." replace="Gun training 2"}

::ab[Gun Training (Ex)]{l=13 icon=down order=nc note="This is identical to the normal gunslinger ability of the same name, except the buccaneer gains it late, and only chooses a single type of firearm." choice="The buccaneer selects one specific type of firearm (such as an ‹eq-weapon/axe musket›, ‹eq-weapon/blunderbuss›, ‹eq-weapon/musket›, or ‹eq-weapon/pistol›). She gain a bonus equal to her Dexterity modifier on damage rolls when firing that type of firearm. Furthermore, when she misfires with that type of firearm, the misfire value of that firearm increases by 2 instead of 4." replace="Gun training 3"}

::ab[Raider's Riposte (Ex)]{l=17 icon=lower passive="Whenever an enemy misses a buccaneer with an attack of opportunity, it provokes an attack of opportunity from the buccaneer." replace="Gun training 4"}

bushwhacker.name: Bushwhacker
description: ## Bushwhacker
‹SOURCE Advanced Race Guide/134›

The bushwhacker specializes in the art of the ambush. For her, gunplay works best when it comes from a concealed position and is directed against a target that falls with the very first volley and is dead before the smoke clears. A bushwhacker has the following class features.
::prereq{r=Kobold}

::ab[Trembling Grit (Ex)]{l=1 icon=down-power order=na note="At the start of each day, a bushwhacker gains grit points equal to her Wisdom modifier - 1 (minimum of 1)." ability="In addition to all the ways any gunslinger can regain grit, a bushwhacker who reduces a helpless or unaware opponent to 0 hit points or fewer also regains 1 grit point. This expands the way in which the bushwhacker gains grit points, so a bushwhacker who gains grit points in this way only gains 1 grit point for reducing a helpless or unaware opponent, not 2 grit points. This otherwise works like the gunslinger's *grit* ability." alter=Grit}
::ab[Craven Deeds (Ex)]{head icon=info note="A bushwhacker replaces some of her deeds with the following deeds. This ability otherwise works like the gunslinger's *deed* ability." alter=Deeds}

::ab[Shifty Shot (Ex)]{next l=3 icon=boost ability="A bushwhacker with at least 1 grit point deals 1d6 points of extra damage when using her firearm to attack opponents denied their Dexterity bonus to AC (whether the target actually has a Dexterity bonus or not)." special="Except for the requirement that she must use a firearm and must have at least 1 grit point to use it, this deed otherwise works identically to a rogue's ‹ability/sneak attack› class feature (including the requirement that she must be within 30 feet of her target to deal this extra damage), and stacks with the *sneak shot* ability (see below) and *sneak attack.*" replace="‹deed/Utility shot› deed"}

::ab[Long Range Shifty Shot (Ex)]{next l=7 icon=boost ability="A bushwhacker can double the range at which she deals extra damage from a *sneak attack* or *shifty shot* (from 30 feet to 60 feet) with a firearm by spending 1 grit point." special="When she spends this grit point, she must still have at least 1 grit point left to deal extra damage from *shifty shot* if she is using this deed." replace="‹deed/Targeting› deed"}

::ab[Sneak Shot (Ex)]{l=4 icon=boost ability="A bushwhacker deals extra damage when using a firearm to attack opponents who are unable to properly defend themselves. The extra damage is a number of d6s equal to ‹misc/one-fourth› of her gunslinger level." special="Unlike with the *shifty shot* ability, she deals this extra damage regardless of whether or not she has any grit points when making the attack. Except for the requirement that she makes a firearm attack, this works identically to a rogue's *sneak attack* class feature. This extra damage stacks with *sneak attack* and the damage from *shifty shot.*" replace="Bonus feats the gunslinger gains at 4th, 8th, 12th, 16th, and 20th level"}

commando.name: Commando
description: ## Commando
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/91›
Commandos are those rough and ready gunslingers who excel at hit-and-run tactics and leveraging their expertise in the wild against their enemies.
::cskill{gain="Knowledge (geography)~Knowledge (nature)~Stealth" lose="Knowledge (engineering), Knowledge (local), Sleight of Hand"}
::ab[Deeds]{head icon=power ability="A commando gains the following deeds; each of these deeds replaces an existing deed."}

::ab[Trap Shot (Ex)]{next l=3 icon=ranged ability="The commando can spend 1 point of grit and target a 5-foot square with her firearm. If she succeeds at an attack against an AC of 10, she triggers a single trap within that 5-foot square, even if she is unaware of the trap. If a creature is occupying the same square as the triggered trap, the trap affects the creature as if it had triggered the trap." special="If there are multiple traps within the same 5-foot square, the commando must declare which trap she is triggering; otherwise it is determined randomly." replace="‹deed/Utility shot› deed"}

::ab[Sniping Shot (Ex)]{next l=11 icon=boost swift="The commando can spend 1 point of grit to negate the -20 penalty on Stealth checks when sniping until the start of her next turn." replace="‹deed/Expert loading› deed"}

::ab[Camouflage (Ex)]{next l=11 icon=power ability="As long as the commando has 1 point of grit, she gains the *camouflage* ‹class/ranger› class feature: She can use the Stealth skill to hide in any of her favored terrains, even if the terrain doesn't grant ‹rule/cover› or ‹rule/concealment›." replace="‹deed/Lightning reload› deed"}

::ab[Hide in Plain Sight (Ex)]{next l=15 icon=power ability="As long as the commando has 1 point of grit, she gains the *hide in plain sight* ranger class feature: While in any of her favored terrains, she can use the Stealth skill even while being observed." replace="‹deed/Slinger's luck› deed"}

::ab[Favored Terrain (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power ability="A commando gains the ‹ability/favored terrain› ranger class feature." imp6="The commando can select a second favored terrain." imp10="She can pick a third favored terrain." imp14="A commando can pick a fourth favored terrain." imp18="The commando can select a fifth favored terrain." replace=Nimble}
::ab[Track (Ex)]{l=4 icon=boost passive="A commando adds half her level on Survival checks to follow tracks." replace="The bonus feat gained at 4th level"}

::ab[Trapsmith]{l=5 icon=power ability="A commando can learn how to create one ‹ability/ranger trap<s› in place of gaining the next level of gun training. A commando can use a known trap a number of times per day equal to her Wisdom bonus (minimum 1). The DC for a commando's trap is equal to 10 + half her commando level + her Wisdom bonus, and it lasts 1 day per 2 levels she has." special="A commando can set only extraordinary ranger traps with this ability; as with extraordinary ranger traps, the trap DC for these traps has a -2 penalty." alter="Gun training"}

experimental_gunsmith.name: Experimental Gunsmith
description: ## Experimental Gunsmith
‹SOURCE Advanced Race Guide/34›
The experimental gunsmith is obsessed with creating the ultimate firearm, and is willing to take risks to work the kinks out of her design. An experimental gunsmith has the following class features.
::prereq{r=Gnome}

::ab[Experimental Firearm (Ex)]{l=1 icon=power ability="An experimental gunsmith begins play with an experimental firearm. This acts as the gunslinger's gunsmith ability, but the firearm the experimental gunsmith begins with has one *innovation* (see below). Further, the experimental gunsmith is treated as having the ‹feat/Gunsmithing› feat, but only in regard to his beginning firearm (or the construction of a replacement firearm of the same design, if the original is lost). This ability otherwise works like the standard gunslinger's abilities." replace=Gunsmith}

:::ab{head title="Innovations (Ex)" icon=power action=Ability replace="Gun training" imp5="The gunslinger learns a second innovation." imp9="The gunslinger learns a third innovation." imp13="She learns a fourth innovation." imp17="She learns a fifth innovation." special="Once an innovation has been selected, it cannot be changed."}

An experimental gunsmith starts play with one innovation she can make to her experimental firearms, selected from the list below. This innovation is a radical change to the typical design of a firearm, and comes with both additional benefits and noteworthy drawbacks. The experimental gunsmith can maintain only one experimental firearm modified with one or more innovations, as it requires constant adjustments and much of her time is spent maintaining the weapon.

If the experimental gunsmith takes the Gunsmithing feat, she can create other firearms using a single innovation she knows (or add one innovation to an existing firearm), though such weapons become unreliable over time. The misfire value of such weapons increases by 1 for each week after its creation, although the experimental gunsmith can return the misfire rate to normal by spending 1 day maintaining the weapon and spending 50 gp in raw materials. For the purposes of crafting such firearms, each innovation is treated as the masterwork weapon quality (with a value of 300 gp).

As he gains levels, the experimental gunsmith learns additional innovations from the list below. These represent constant and increasingly skilled tinkering with a single firearm in her possession, and when the experimental gunsmith gains an innovation, she may immediately apply it to her experimental firearm.

::ab[Expanded Capacity]{next icon=boost-down ability="The experimental gunsmith increases her firearm's capacity by one (generally by adding another barrel, depending on the weapon's design). She also increases its weight by 50%. Because of the complexity of increasing its capacity, her weapon is more prone to misfire, and its misfire value increases by 1 (this is in addition to any misfire increase if the weapon is made with the Gunsmithing feat)."}

::ab[Expanded Chamber]{next icon=boost-down ability="The experimental gunsmith's firearm has a much larger than normal chamber for black powder, allowing her to use 2 doses of powder with each shot. The weapon grants a +1 circumstance bonus on damage rolls. However, when the weapon misfires, it causes a flashburn. This is the same as the weapon exploding (dealing damage from a corner of the experimental gunsmith's square), except the weapon is not automatically destroyed. If a ‹misc/broken› weapon misfires, it is destroyed as normal."}

::ab[Grapple Launcher]{next icon=power-down ability="The experimental gunsmith's firearm includes a special set of rails that allows her to slide a grappling hook over the barrel and fire it as a @HLfull-round_action. The grappling hook anchors into anything she successfully hits with an attack roll, and the DC of the Strength check required to pull the grappling hook loose is equal to her attack roll to lodge it in place. A Disable Device check can remove it with a +5 bonus on the check, and a creature struck by the hook can escape it with a combat maneuver check or Escape Artist check with a +5 bonus on the check. The firearm is powerful enough to fire a hook trailing up to 100 feet of rope, but throws off the balance of the experimental gunsmith's firearm, reducing its range increment by 50%."}

::ab[Recoilless]{next icon=boost-down ability="The experimental gunsmith's firearm includes a series of gears, springs, and pistons designed to reduce its recoil and thus improve accuracy. The range increment of the weapon increases by 10 feet. However, the system is fragile, and easily damaged. Anytime the weapon misfires, this system becomes broken and does not function until the experimental gunsmith spends 1 hour repairing it."}

::ab[Vial Launcher]{next icon=power-down ability="The experimental gunsmith adds a special tube onto her firearm that can hold 1 vial of alchemical material, such as ‹eq-weapon/alchemist's fire›, a ‹eq-weapon/tanglefoot bag›, or a ‹eq-weapon/thunderstone›. A special trigger allows her to channel the force of black powder into this tube to launch the vial as a ranged attack with half the range increment of her firearm. The experimental gunsmith can take this action in place of any single ranged attack, and may use the same attack bonus that she can apply to the firearm. Reloading the tube is a time-consuming process and requires two @HLfull-round_actions. If the weapon misfires while loaded with an alchemical substance, it detonates with the experimental gunsmith (and the firearm) as the target." special="If the alchemical substance has a save DC, it becomes 10 + 1/2 her experimental gunsmith level + her Wisdom modifier when she launches the vial from her firearm. This increase only applies to the initial saving throw. If additional saving throws are called for, they are resolved as normal."}

firebrand.name: Firebrand
description: ## Firebrand
‹SOURCE Elemental Master's Handbook/4›
Where other gunslingers rely on precision shooting, the firebrand instead masters explosive ordnance. Firebrands tend to be passionate, devil-may-care wanderers living each day to the fullest.
::ab[Gunsmith]{l=1 icon=power ability="A firebrand gains a ‹eq-weapon/dragon pistol› as her battered firearm." alter=Gunsmith}

::ab[Wild Card (Ex)]{icon=info order=np note="A firebrand uses her Charisma instead of Wisdom to determine the number of grit points she gains at the start of each day and to determine the DCs of her gunslinger deeds." passive="A firebrand treats direct hits with her bombs as firearm attacks for the purpose of regaining grit (see the *bombs* ability below)." special="This ability works in all other ways like the gunslinger's *grit* class feature." alter=Grit}
::ab[Deeds]{head icon=power ability="A firebrand gains the following deeds."}

::ab[Dragon Fire (Ex)]{next icon=boost flavor="A firebrand is a master of the volatile substance known as dragon's breath." l=1 swift="The firebrand can spend 1 grit point to add her Charisma modifier to the DCs of all ‹eq-weapon/dragon's breath cartridge«s› she fires until the end of her turn." passive="Her attacks with dragon's breath cartridges misfire only if a 1 is rolled on two or more of the damage dice." order=wp replace="‹deed/Deadeye› deed"}

::ab[Big Boom! (Su)]{next l=7 icon=ranged flavor="A firebrand can channel her explosive potential into a mighty blast." standard="The firebrand shoots a 30-foot cone of fire from the barrel of her gun. The firebrand designates one creature in the squares affected by the cone to be the target and makes an attack roll with her firearm against that creature; on a hit, the target takes damage as if it were directly hit by both the firebrand's firearm and her bomb. Creatures in all other squares in the cone (as well as the target's square, if the gunslinger's attack missed) take splash damage as if they were in the area of the firebrand's bomb, with a Reflex saving throw for half damage. (The DC of this saving throw is equal to 10 + half the firebrand's gunslinger level + the firebrand's Charisma modifier.)" special="Using this deed costs 2 grit points and expends one of the firebrand's daily uses of bombs (see the *bombs* ability below), as well as the appropriate ammunition for the firearm attack; ammunition that does not require an attack roll cannot be used this way." replace="‹deed/Dead shot› deed"}

::ab[Scorched Earth (Ex)]{flavor="The firebrand knows how to make dragon's breath burn white-hot." icon=boost l=4 passive="The fire damage of the first dragon's breath cartridge fired by the firebrand each round increases by a number of d6s equal to ‹misc/one-fourth› of her gunslinger level (to a maximum total of 7d6 at 20th level)." replace="Bonus feats gained at 4th, 8th, 12th, 16th, and 20th levels"}

::ab[Bombs (Su)]{l=5 icon=zap ability="The firebrand gains the bombs ability as an ‹class/alchemist› of her gunslinger level - 4, using her Charisma modifier in place of her Intelligence modifier to determine her number of bombs per day and the DC to avoid splash damage. The firebrand adds her Charisma modifier to her bombs' damage instead of her Intelligence modifier, even if she has the *throw anything* class feature. In addition, the firebrand's bombs are always under the effect of the ‹discovery/explosive bomb› discovery." special="Firebrand levels do not stack with levels in other classes with the bombs class feature." replace="Gun training"}

graveslinger.name: Graveslinger
description: ## Graveslinger
‹SOURCE Pathfinder #139: The Dead Road/65›
Graveslingers craft signature bullets to hunt down wandering undead. With this infused ammunition, they can take on the most resilient spectral foes with ease.
::ab[Deeds]{head icon=info note="A graveslinger gains the following deeds."}

::ab[Ghostbane Shot (Ex)]{next l=1 icon=power-boost ability="Once per day by spending 10 minutes to combine mundane ammunition with a mixture of grave dirt and powdered bone, the graveslinger can prepare a number of special bullets equal to her graveslinger level plus her Wisdom modifier (minimum 1). When attacking incorporeal undead, these bullets are treated as if they had the ‹magic-enh/ghost touch› special ability." special="These bullets function only for the graveslinger, and if not fired within 24 hours, the bullets become inert, losing their ghost touch special ability." replace="‹deed/Deadeye› deed"}

::ab[Ectoplasmic Anchor (Su)]{next l=7 icon=lower ability="An incorporeal undead creature hit by one of the graveslinger's *ghostbane shots* becomes infused with ectoplasm, interfering with its defenses and mobility. The creature must succeed at a Will save (DC = 10 + half the graveslinger's level + the graveslinger's Wisdom modifier) or become semi-corporeal for 1 round." order=an note="A semi-corporeal creature takes half damage (50%) from nonmagical attack forms, and it takes full damage from magic weapons, spells, spell-like effects, and supernatural effects. During this time, it cannot enter or pass through solid objects." replace="‹deed/Startling shot› deed"}

::ab[Undead Hunter (Ex)]{next l=11 icon=boost passive="The graveslinger's *ghostbane shots* are treated as if they also had the ‹magic-enh/undead »bane› special ability." replace="‹deed/Bleeding wound› deed"}

::ab[Staggering Shot (Ex)]{next l=15 icon=lower passive="When a graveslinger confirms a critical hit against an undead creature with one of her *ghostbane shots,* the creature is ‹misc/staggered› for 1 round." replace="‹deed/Menacing shot› deed"}

::ab[Supernatural Awareness (Ex)]{l=4 icon=power ability="The graveslinger gains ‹feat/Blind-Fight› as a bonus feat." order=ap passive="As long as she has 1 grit point, she can always act in surprise rounds that are triggered by haunts and incorporeal undead, even if she fails to notice them." replace="The bonus feat gained at 4th level"}

gulch_gunner.name: Gulch Gunner
description: ## Gulch Gunner
‹SOURCE Advanced Race Guide/152›

Ratfolk warfare often occurs in cramped, claustrophobic spaces such as the ratfolk's own underground warrens and heavily trapped gulch fortresses, or the subterranean tunnels of goblins and similar foes. Since these battlegrounds often have tight corners and few areas with long lines of sight, those ratfolk who seek to master the use of firearms almost inevitably become experts in close-quarters combat. These gulch gunners often specialize in proficiency with a single pistol (easily handled in tight spaces) and wander from warren to warren selling their tunnel-shooting skills to the highest bidder. A gulch gunner has the following class features.
::prereq{r=Ratfolk}
::cskill{gain="Disable Device~Escape Artist~Knowledge (dungeoneering)" lose="Knowledge (local), Ride, Swim"}

::ab[Grit (Ex)]{head l=1 icon=info note="A gulch gunner gains grit as a standard gunslinger. However, the close-combat focus of the gulch gunner results in a slightly different set of circumstances for regaining grit." alter=Grit}

::ab[Daring Adjacent Shot]{next icon=power ability="The first time each round the gulch gunner makes a ranged firearm attack against an adjacent foe and provokes an attack of opportunity, she regains 1 grit point. Making an adjacent firearm attack against a ‹misc/helpless› or unaware creature or on a creature that has fewer Hit Dice than half the gulch gunner's character level does not restore grit, nor do ranged attacks using some ability that prevents the gulch gunner from provoking an attack of opportunity." replace="The ability to regain grit from a critical hit with a firearm"}

::ab[Deeds]{head icon=info note="A gulch gunner has some unique deeds, which replace some of the standard gunslinger deeds as listed in each entry. These deeds follow all the general rules and restrictions of gunslinger deeds."}

::ab[Flash and Shock (Ex)]{next l=1 icon=boost flavor="The gulch gunner can take advantage of the flash and sound of a firearm to throw off an attacker's aim at close range." ability="When she makes an attack against a foe within her firearm's first range increment, she may spend 1 grit point to gain a +4 circumstance bonus to AC against that opponent until the beginning of her next turn." special="She can only perform this deed while wearing medium, light, or no armor, and while carrying no more than a light load." replace="‹deed/Deadeye› deed"}

::ab[Powder Burns (Ex)]{next l=3 icon=boost flavor="The gulch gunner learns to maximize the damage dealt by a firearm at extremely close range." ability="When the gulch gunner successfully hits an adjacent foe with a ranged firearm attack, she may spend 1 grit point to deal an additional 1d6 points of fire damage from the muzzle flash of her weapon. The target must also make a Reflex save (DC 10 + 1/2 the gulch gunner's level + the gulch gunner's Wisdom modifier) or ‹rule/catch on fire›." replace="‹deed/Pistol whip› deed"}

::ab[Staggering Shot (Ex)]{next l=15 icon=lower passive="When the gulch gunner hits an adjacent target with a ranged firearm attack, the target must also make a Fortitude save (DC 10 + 1/2 the gulch gunner's level + the gulch gunner's Wisdom modifier) or be ‹misc/staggered› for 1d4 rounds." replace="‹deed/Menacing shot› deed"}

::ab[Belly Shot (Ex)]{l=9 icon=boost flavor="A gulch gunner can press her firearm directly against a vulnerable location of an adjacent target, dealing additional damage." ability="When the gulch gunner successfully hits an adjacent target with a ranged firearm attack, she deals +1d6 points of damage." special="Creatures immune to ‹misc/precision damage›, critical hits, or sneak attacks do not take this additional damage." increment="The extra damage~9~4" incrementEnd="d6." replace="Gun training ability gained at 9th, 13th, and 17th level"}

gun_scavenger.name: Gun Scavenger
description: ## Gun Scavenger
‹SOURCE Inner Sea Combat/38›

Although Alkenstar is the center of firearm production and innovation in the Inner Sea, the crack of flint and tang of gun smoke is enough to inspire enthusiasts elsewhere to try their hand at making their own guns using intuition instead of blueprints. A gun scavenger collects scraps, spare parts, and other components that might - if modified properly - give her firearm an extra edge.

::ab[Gunsmith]{icon=info note="The gun scavenger's starting weapon is a mishmash of pieces that even other gunpowder enthusiasts fail to comprehend. Anyone other than the gun scavenger treats her starting weapon as ‹misc/broken›, even if she uses the ‹feat/Gunsmithing› feat to repair it. This ability works in all other ways like the gunslinger's *gunsmith* class feature." alter=Gunsmith}

::ab[Go By Feel (Ex)]{icon=down flavor="A gun scavenger's intuitive and unique style leaves her firearms prone to misfire, whether as a result of using too much powder, using imperfectly milled parts, or any number of other minor inconsistencies." note="When using a firearm, a gun scavenger cannot reduce that firearm's misfire chance to 0; it can always misfire on a natural 1."}
::ab[Deeds]{head icon=power note="A gun scavenger gains the following deeds at the listed level."}

:::ab{next l=1 icon=power action="Full-Round Action" special="A gun scavenger must have at least 1 grit point to perform this deed. Alternatively, if the gun scavenger spends 1 grit point to perform a *change out,* she can either perform the *change out* as a @HLstandard_action instead of a full-round action, or perform the *change out* on a firearm that isn't broken." title="Change Out (Ex)" replace="‹deed/Quick clear› deed"}

A gun scavenger can remove the broken condition from a single firearm she is currently wielding, as long as that condition was gained by a firearm misfire. When she does, she can replace the broken part with a specialized, short-lived component that does one of the following.
- Gives the firearm the ‹misc/scatter› weapon quality
- Increases the damage dealt by the ‹deed/pistol-whip› deed by one die size
- Increases the firearm's range increment by 10 feet.
A firearm with such a modification increases its misfire chance cumulatively by 1 each time it is fired until it misfires. When it does misfire, the effects of the temporary component are lost.

::ab[Jury-Rig (Ex)]{next l=7 icon=power-magic standard="A gun scavenger can adjust a firearm she is currently wielding to fire in an unconventional way. She can spend 1 grit point to grant her firearm one of the following magic weapon enhancements for 1 round per gunslinger level: ‹magic-enh/distance›, ‹magic-enh/flaming›, ‹magic-enh/glamered<_w›, or ‹magic-enh/thundering›. If she spends 2 grit points, she can instead grant the firearm ‹magic-enh/flaming burst› or ‹magic-enh/stalking›." special="Multiple uses of this ability don't stack with themselves." replace="‹deed/Targeting› deed"}

::ab[Arbitrary Aim (Ex)]{l=2 icon=lower flavor="A gun scavenger's attacks with firearms are difficult to predict." passive="When shooting a firearm at a creature that has a dodge or insight bonus to its Armor Class, the gun scavenger treats the combined bonus as if it were 1 lower." incrementPlain="The reduction~2~4" replace=Nimble}

gun_tank.name: Gun Tank
description: ## Gun Tank
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/50›

Gunslingers usually don't use heavy armor, but there are a few who use and modify their armor to protect themselves from both traditional weapons and gunfire. These gun tanks trundle into battle with barrels blazing, dealing out punishment with their firearms while knowing few can penetrate their hard protective shells.
::ab[Armor Proficiency]{icon=power ability="A gun tank is proficient with all armors and shields, including tower shields."}
::ab[Deeds]{head icon=info note="A gun tank swaps a pair of deeds for the following."}

::ab[Gun Tank's Resolve (Ex)]{next l=1 icon=def flavor="The gun tank can shrug off the most serious of attacks." immediate="When the gun tank is wearing medium or heavy armor and is subject to a critical hit or ‹ability/sneak attack›, she can spend 1 grit point to attempt to negate the critical hit or sneak attack damage. She has a 25% chance of doing so." imp10="The negation chance becomes 50%." imp15="The chance increases to 75%." special="While a gun tank's resolve does not stack with the ‹magic-enh/light_>fortification› armor special ability, it does work in concert with that armor special ability or similar effects, so a gun tank can use this ability even after the armor of fortification has failed to negate the critical hit or sneak attack damage." replace="‹deed/Gunslinger's dodge› deed"}

::ab[Gun Tank's Resilience (Ex)]{next l=15 icon=def passive="When the gun tank has at least 1 grit point and makes a successful Fortitude saving throw against an attack that would deal half damage or have a partial effect, she takes no damage or other effects from that attack. Furthermore, she gains a +2 bonus on all saving throws against fear and mind-affecting effects." replace="‹deed/Evasive› deed"}

::ab[Bullet Defection (Ex)]{icon=def flavor="A gun tank is adept at modifying and using her armor to stop firearm attacks." l=2 passive="A gun tank wearing medium or heavy armor gains half the armor's bonus plus the armor's enhancement bonus (if any) as a deflection bonus against any non-siege firearm or splash weapon attack (including the ‹class/alchemist«'s› bomb class ability). This ability has no affect on spells, spell-like abilities, or supernatural abilities that make a touch attack." replace=Nimble}

::ab[Armor Training (Ex)]{l=4 icon=boost-def flavor="A gun tank learns to be more maneuverable while wearing armor." passive="Whenever she is wearing armor, the armor check penalty is reduced by ‹misc/one-fourth› of her gunslinger level (to a minimum of 0) and the maximum Dexterity bonus allowed by her armor increases by one-fourth of her gunslinger level." special="This ability stacks with the ‹class/fighter› class ability of the same name. If the gun tank has both class features, she takes the most advantageous benefit of the two class features." replace="All bonus combat/grit feats"}

gunner_squire.name: Gunner Squire
description: ## Gunner Squire
‹SOURCE Knights of the Inner Sea/20›
Prepared to reload or repair her lord's firearms at a moment's notice, the gunner squire is an indispensable aid for knights skilled in the use of pistols and rifles.
::ab[Deeds]{head icon=info note="A gunner squire swaps a pair of deeds for the following."}

::ab[Spotter (Ex)]{next l=1 icon=boost standard="A gunner squire can spend 1 grit point to pinpoint a target within her line of sight and inform her knightly liege of the target's exact location. During the knight's next turn, if he makes a ranged attack against the pinpointed target and would take a penalty on his attack roll due to range, he may reduce this penalty by an amount equal to his gunner squire's Wisdom modifier (to a minimum penalty of 0)." special="The gunner squire must be adjacent to her liege and able to communicate to use this deed, though a knight may move away from the gunner squire during his turn and still gain the benefits of this deed." replace="‹deed/Gunslinger's dodge› deed"}

::ab[Side Arm (Ex)]{next l=3 icon=power fullround="A gunner squire can spend 1 grit point to reload a one-handed or two-handed firearm she is holding. If the firearm has multiple barrels, she can reload a number of barrels per round equal to her Dexterity modifier (minimum 1). A gunner squire equipped with alchemical cartridges can use this deed to reload multiple firearms at once, dividing the number of barrels she's able to reload among the firearms she is carrying however she wishes, even if this would mean stowing and retrieving multiple firearms from her equipment with one action. Abilities, deeds, and feats that decrease reloading time (such as ‹feat/Rapid Reload›) do not improve this deed." replace="‹deed/Gunslinger initiative›"}

::ab[Safe Handling (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def fullround="A gunner squire can temporarily ensure the safety of a firearm she is holding, reducing its misfire chance by 1 (to a minimum of 0) on the next attack roll made with that firearm. This reduction does not stack with itself. This reduction occurs after any increases are calculated for firing with the ‹misc/broken› condition, or for any other effect that might increase the misfire value of a firearm." replace="Nimble +1"}

maverick.name: Maverick
description: ## Maverick
‹SOURCE Ultimate Intrigue/64›
Trouble always finds a maverick, whether she is a con artist or an honest gambler. Quick with her gun and no stranger to barroom brawls, she can hold her own when bullets and punches start flying.
::ab[Deeds]{head icon=power note="A maverick gains the following three deeds."}

::ab[Stacked Deck (Ex)]{next l=1 icon=boost ability="The maverick can spend 1 grit point when she attempts a Bluff, Profession (gambler), or Sleight of Hand check to roll 1d6 and add the result to the check. She can do this after she rolls the check but before the result is revealed. If the result of the d6 roll is a natural 6, she rolls another 1d6 and adds it to the check. She can continue to do this as long as she rolls natural 6s, up to a number of times equal to her Wisdom modifier (minimum 1)." replace="‹deed/Gunslinger's dodge› deed"}

::ab[Fist Fighter (Ex)]{next l=3 icon=power ability="As long as the maverick has at least 1 grit point, she gains the benefit of the ‹feat/Improved Unarmed Strike› feat." replace="‹deed/Pistol whip› deed"}

::ab[Gun Twirl (Ex)]{next l=3 icon=power ability="As long as the maverick has at least 1 grit point, she can use flashy gunplay to intimidate her opponents. She gains the ‹feat/Dazzling Display› feat and can use it with any firearm, even if she doesn't have ‹feat/Weapon Focus› in that firearm." replace="‹deed/Gunslinger initiative› deed"}

musket_master.name: Musket Master
description: ## Musket Master
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/50›

Pistols and other one-handed firearms are often easy to conceal and faster to load than other firearms, and do sufficient damage for most situations. But some gunslingers want a little more firepower and range. These are the musket masters; the best of these gunslingers can reload a musket with dazzling speed, potentially putting even their pistol-wielding brethren to shame.

::ab[Weapon Proficiency]{icon=down note="Instead of proficiency with all firearms, a musket master only gains proficiency with two-handed firearms. She must take the ‹feat/Exotic Weapon Proficiency› (firearm) feat to gain proficiency with one-handed firearms and firearm siege engines."}
::ab[Gunsmith]{icon=info note="A musket master must take a ‹eq-weapon/musket› when she chooses a battered firearm at 1st level." alter=Gunsmith}
::ab[Deeds]{head icon=info note="A musket master swaps a pair of deeds for the following."}

::ab[Steady Aim (Ex)]{next l=1 icon=boost move="As long as a musket master has at least 1 grit point, she can increase the accuracy of a two-handed firearm. When she does, she increases the range increment of the firearm she is firing by 10 feet. This stacks with other abilities that increase her range increment." replace="‹deed/Gunslinger's dodge› deed"}

::ab[Fast Musket (Ex)]{next l=3 icon=power ability="As long as the musket master has 1 grit point, she can reload any two-handed firearm as if it were a one-handed firearm." replace="‹deed/Utility shot› deed"}
::ab[Rapid Reloader]{l=1 icon=power ability="A musket master gains ‹feat/Rapid Reload› (muskets) as a bonus feat."}

::ab[Musket Training (Ex)]{l=5 icon=boost-def ability="A musket master increases her skill with two-handed firearms. She gains a bonus on damage rolls equal to her Dexterity modifier, and when she misfires with a two-handed firearm, the misfire value increases by 2 instead of 4." increment="The damage bonus~5~4~1" replace="Firearm training 1, 2, 3, and 4"}

mysterious_stranger.name: Mysterious Stranger
description: ## Mysterious Stranger
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/51›

Gunslingers use a potent mixture of common sense and manual dexterity in order to control the fickle power of their weapons. But a few rare gunslingers seem to accomplish their chosen tasks purely through willpower and an unwillingness to ever give up. These mysterious strangers use their force of personality to keep going when the chips are down, often moving on from a region as soon as they accomplish their goals - hence the name.

::ab[Grit (Ex)]{icon=info note="A mysterious stranger is a force to be reckoned with. Instead of using her Wisdom to determine the number of grit points she gains at the start of each day, she uses Charisma. This ability works in all other ways like the gunslinger's *grit* class feature." alter=Grit}
::ab[Deeds]{head icon=info note="A mysterious stranger swaps a pair of deeds for the following."}

::ab[Focused Aim (Ex)]{next l=1 icon=boost swift="The mysterious stranger can spend 1 grit point to gain a bonus on all firearm damage rolls equal to her Charisma modifier (minimum 1) with all firearm attacks she makes until the end of her turn." imp7="When she uses the ‹deed/dead shot› deed, she multiplies this bonus by the number of hits she made while rolling the *Dead Shot* attack." replace="‹deed/Quick clear› deed"}

::ab[Clipping Shot (Ex)]{next l=11 icon=ranged ability="When the mysterious stranger misses with a firearm attack, she can spend 1 grit point to deal half the damage that attack would have dealt if it were a hit (roll damage normally). She can decide to use this deed and spend the grit point after making the attack. This deed has no effect if the attack used the *dead shot* deed." special="The cost of using this deed cannot be reduced with the ‹feat/Signature Deed› feat, the true grit class feature, or any similar effect." replace="‹deed/Bleeding wound› deed"}
::ab[Lucky (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def passive="A mysterious stranger gains a +1 luck bonus on Will saving throws." increment="This bonus~2~4" replace=Nimble}
::ab[Stranger's Fortune (Ex)]{l=5 icon=def free="A mysterious stranger can ignore a firearm misfire." useMod=Cha replace="Gun training 1"}

pistolero.name: Pistolero
description: ## Pistolero
**Source** ‹source/Ultimate Combat« pg. 51›

While most gunslingers have favorite firearms, there are those rare ones who choose to specialize in one-handed firearms exclusively. These gunslingers are called pistoleros. While they lack the flexibility of being able to pick up any kind of firearm and use it with reasonable proficiency, they are crackerjack shots with pistols and similar firearms. Their skill and aim with these firearms at close range is second to none, and they rarely misfire with these weapons.

::ab[Weapon Proficiency]{icon=down note="Instead of proficiency with all firearms, a pistolero only gains proficiency with one-handed firearms. She must take ‹feat/Exotic Weapon Proficiency› (firearm) to gain proficiency with two-handed firearms and firearm siege engines."}
::ab[Gunsmith]{icon=info note="A pistolero must take a ‹eq-weapon/pistol› when she chooses a battered firearm at 1st level." alter=Gunsmith}
::ab[Deeds]{head icon=info note="A pistolero swaps a trio of deeds for the following."}

::ab[Up Close and Deadly (Ex)]{next l=1 icon=boost ability="When the pistolero hits a target with a one-handed firearm that is not making a scatter shot, she can spend 1 grit point to deal extra damage on a hit. This extra damage is a number of d6s equal to 1 + ‹misc/one-fifth› of her gunslinger level. If she misses with the attack, she grazes the target, dealing half the extra damage anyway. This is ‹misc/precision damage› and is not multiplied if the attack is a critical hit. This precision damage stacks with ‹ability/sneak attack› and other forms of precision damage." special="The cost of using this deed cannot be reduced with the ‹feat/Signature Deed› feat, the true grit class feature, or any similar effect." replace="*Deadeye* deed"}
::ab[Deadeye (Ex)]{next l=7 icon=power ability="The pistolero gains the ‹deed/deadeye› deed, which is normally a 1st-level gunslinger deed." replace="‹deed/Startling shot› deed"}

::ab[Twin Shot Knockdown (Ex)]{next l=11 icon=boost ability="When the pistolero hits a single target with two or more one-handed firearm attacks during her turn, she can spend 1 grit point to knock the target ‹rule/prone›. She can choose to spend the grit point after the attacks are made." replace="‹deed/Bleeding wound› deed"}

::ab[Pistol Training (Ex)]{l=5 icon=boost passive="A pistolero increases her skill with one-handed firearms. She gains a bonus on damage rolls equal to her Dexterity modifier, and when she misfires with a one-handed firearm, the misfire value increases by 2 instead of 4." increment="The bonus on damage rolls~5~4" replace="Gun training 1-4"}

planar_rifter.name: Planar Rifter
description: ## Planar Rifter
‹SOURCE Plane-Hopper's Handbook/6›

Followers of the daemonic harbinger ‹faith/Cixyron› invented a method of harvesting pieces from Abaddon, absorbing some of the energy into themselves and condensing other portions of it into bullets they could fire from their guns. While these methods originated among Cixyron's followers, they have spread among a few other seasoned planar travelers. Not all planar rifters are evil, as the same methods that proved effective in slaying the celestial host are just as useful against other planar forces.

::ab[Infused Grit (Su)]{icon=magic ability="Each day when a planar rifter recovers grit, he can attune his grit pool to one of the following subtypes: air, chaotic, earth, evil, fire, good, lawful, or water." standard="Whenever he is on a plane that has an alignment or elemental trait, he can switch his attunement to match one of that plane's traits." free="After reducing an outsider to 0 hit points or below, he can switch his attunement to match one of that outsider's subtypes." order=asf special="He regains grit as normal from critical hits, but killing blows replenish his grit pool only if the target is an outsider." alter=Grit}
::ab[Deeds]{head icon=info note="A planar rifter gains the following deeds."}
:::ab{next title="Infused Bullet (Su)" l=1 icon=magic action=Ability replace="‹deed/Deadeye› deed"}

A planar rifter's bullets carry the essence of a plane to which his grit pool is attuned, striking true against creatures that shrink from that plane's nature. As long as he has at least 1 point in his grit pool, he gains the following benefits.
- If he infuses his bullets with an alignment, his bullet's damage counts as that alignment for the purposes of overcoming DR
- If he infuses his bullets with an element, half of his bullet's damage changes to an energy type determined by the element infused: acid (earth), cold (water), electricity (air), or fire (fire)

::ab[Planar Surge (Su)]{next l=7 icon=magic swift="A planar rifter can spend 1 grit point to change the attunement of his grit pool to any alignment or element. This new attunement remains for 1 minute, after which time the grit pool becomes unattuned." replace="‹deed/Dead shot› deed"}

::ab[Breaching Shot (Su)]{next l=15 icon=magic flavor="A planar rifter can fire bullets with such precision that he tears temporary rifts between planes." standard="He can spend 3 grit points to shoot at a location within his firearm's first range increment and tear a 10-foot-radius portal to a plane of his choice. The rift leads to a random location on the plane that is between 5 and 500 miles from his intended destination, as ‹spell/plane shift›. The rift remains open for 1 minute, during which time any number of willing creatures can pass through. It is a one-way rift, and it is insufficiently stable to reveal details about the environment on the other side." replace="‹deed/Menacing shot› deed"}

::ab[Banishing Shot (Su)]{next l=19 icon=magic ability="Whenever a planar rifter confirms a critical hit against an extraplanar target, he can spend 1 grit to deal normal damage, and the target must succeed at a Will saving throw with a DC equal to 20 + the planar rifter's Wisdom modifier. On a failed saving throw, the target is returned to its original plane as per the ‹spell/banishment› spell." replace="‹deed/Death's shot› deed"}

::ab[Planar Resistance (Su)]{l=2 icon=def passive="When a planar rifter infuses his grit with the essence of a plane, he gains protection against that plane's dangers. If he attunes his grit pool to chaos, good, evil, or law, he gains resistance to magical effects that deal damage based on the chosen alignment, such as ‹spell/holy smite›, ‹spell/order's wrath›, a ‹class/paladin«'s› smite evil attack, or an ‹magic-enh/unholy› weapon. If he attunes his grit pool to air, earth, fire, or water, he gains protection against the energy type associated with that element (see *infused bullet*). He reduces the amount of damage taken from attacks of the chosen type by 2 points per gunslinger level." replace=Nimble}

::ab[Planar Strike (Su)]{l=5 icon=boost passive="As long as he has at least 1 point in his grit pool, a planar rifter's weapon attacks deal an extra 1d6 points of damage of the same damage type reduced by his planar resistance. Aligned damage harms only creatures of an opposed alignment." imp13="This damage increases to 2d6." replace="Gun training"}

scatter_gunner.name: Scatter Gunner
description: ## Scatter Gunner
‹SOURCE People of the Wastes/12›

Punishing firearms such as the blunderbuss and dragon pistol can lay waste to invaders in tight corridors, and the dwarves of Dongun Hold have pioneered masterful methods for making the most of these indiscriminate weapons.
::ab[Scatter Specialist]{icon=info note="A scatter gunner must take a ‹eq-weapon/blunderbuss› or ‹eq-weapon/dragon pistol› when he chooses a battered firearm at 1st level." alter=Gunsmith}
::ab[Deeds]{head icon=info note="A scatter gunner gains the following deeds, each of which functions only when he uses a weapon with the ‹misc/scatter› weapon quality to make a scattering shot."}

::ab[Careful Shot (Ex)]{next icon=def order=pa passive="As long as a scatter gunner has at least 1 grit point when making a scattering shot, he takes a penalty equal to his Wisdom modifier on his attack rolls against any allies in the area." ability="He can spend 1 grit point when making a scattering shot to automatically miss a number of creatures equal to his Wisdom modifier." replace="‹deed/Deadeye› deed"}
:::ab{next l=3 icon=boost action=Ability title="Scatter Artist (Ex)" replace="‹deed/Utility shot› deed"}
When using a firearm to make a scattering shot, a scatter gunner can spend 1 grit point to add one of the following effects to his attack.
- @HL[Restricted Barrel:] The scattering shot attacks all creatures in a line rather than in a cone. The line's length is twice the length of the weapon's cone (30 feet for most early firearms).
- @HL[Shaped Blast:] Select one additional square adjacent to the scattering shot's area of effect; creatures in this square are also affected by the scattering shot.

- @HL[Vortex:] The firearm releases an explosive torrent of air in the scattering shot's affected area for 1 round, creating a strong ‹rule/wind› effect (25 mph) that can disperse fog and smoke effects such as those created by ‹spell/fog cloud›.

::ab[Overload (Ex)]{next l=7 icon=boost flavor="A scatter gunner knows just how to hold his weapon to maximize its devastating scattering effects." fullround="A scatter gunner can make a scattering shot to fire a concentrated payload of pellets that strains his firearm. This doubles the area affected by the scattering shot (a 30-foot cone for most early firearms), increases the firearm's misfire value by 2, and causes each successful attack to deal an additional 1d6 points of damage that is not multiplied on a critical hit. The scatter gunner must spend 1 grit point to perform this deed." incrementPlain="The additional damage~7~6" incrementEnd="d6." replace="‹deed/Dead shot› deed"}

::ab[Satchel Shot (Ex)]{next l=7 icon=ranged fullround="A scatter gunner can load and fire an explosive shell, consuming ammunition as though he had made a scattering shot. This functions like ‹rule/throw«ing a» splash weapon› at a grid intersection but uses his firearm's range increment, attack bonus, and magic special abilities (if any). The shell affects creatures in a 10-foot radius as though the scatter gunner had made a scattering shot against them, and damaged creatures must succeed at a Reflex save (DC = 10 + half the scatter gunner's level + the scatter gunner's Dexterity modifier) or ‹rule/catch fire›." replace="‹deed/Targeting› deed"}

siege_gunner.name: Siege Gunner
description: ## Siege Gunner
‹SOURCE Inner Sea Combat/45›

The Grand Duchy of Alkenstar has spawned all manner of firearm specialists, wielding small and large firearms alike. Siege gunners specialize in the very largest: ‹eq-siegeengine/cannon«s›, ‹eq-siegeengine/bombard«s›, and ‹eq-siegeengine/firedrake«s›. These specialists are rarely seen outside of Alkenstar, however, in part because the duchy keeps close tabs on its most powerful guns and few are found elsewhere in the Inner Sea. A few siege gunners outside of Alkenstar make a living renting out their services and the use of their personal collection of siege weaponry to causes in which they believe or to the highest bidder.

::ab[Grit (Ex)]{icon=info note="A siege gunner relies less on luck and intuition and more on cold calculation and wit. The number of grit points a siege gunner gains each day and her maximum grit are based on the siege gunner's Intelligence modifier rather than her Wisdom modifier."}

::ab[Engineer Training (Ex)]{l=2 flavor="A siege gunner is trained in all aspects of siegecraft, including the workings of siege engines and knowledge of the structures that are often the target of their assaults." icon=boost passive="A siege gunner gains a bonus on Knowledge (engineering) checks equal to 1/2 her gunslinger level." replace=Nimble}
::ab[Deeds]{head icon=info note="A siege gunner swaps a pair of deeds of her choosing for the following."}

::ab[Targeted Blast (Ex)]{next l=1 icon=boost ability="When using a firearm or siege engine that targets an area rather than a specific creature, the siege gunner can spend 1 grit point to focus the brunt of the blast on specific creatures within the target area. She can select a number of creatures equal to 1 + ‹misc/one-fifth› of her gunslinger level. The target creatures take 1 additional point of damage for each gunslinger level the siege gunner possesses." special="This is ‹misc/precision damage› and is not multiplied on a critical hit. Targets must be selected before any attack rolls or saving throws are made." replace="‹deed/Deadeye› deed"}

::ab[Scattershot (Ex)]{next l=3 icon=boost ability="If the siege gunner has at least 1 grit point, she can increase the effectiveness of ‹misc/scatter«ing shots› from hand-held firearms and ‹eq-siegeengine/blast shot«s› from siege engines. The cone radius of scattering shots or blast shots increases by 5 feet for every three levels the siege gunner possesses (to a maximum of 30 feet at 18th level)." replace="‹deed/Gunslinger initiative› deed"}

::ab[Bonus Feats]{icon=power l4="A siege gunner gains ‹feat/Siege Engineer› as a bonus feat. She does not need to meet the prerequisites for this feat." l8="She gains ‹feat/Master Siege Engineer› as a bonus feat. She does not need to meet the prerequisites for this feat." l12="The siege gunner gains a bonus feat in addition to those gained by normal advancement. This must be a ‹main/combat feat› or ‹main/grit feat›, and she must meet the prerequisites as normal." l16="She gains another bonus combat or grit feat, as above." l20="She gains a third bonus combat or grit feat, as above." replace="The gunslinger's normal bonus feats"}

techslinger.name: Techslinger
description: ## Techslinger
‹SOURCE Technology Guide/13›

Techslingers spurn unreliable gunpowder weapons in favor of high-tech armaments. They learn to master the quirks of ‹rule/timeworn technology› and methods of keeping weapons charged in a primitive world. Techslingers still start play with the black powder blunderbuss, pistol, or rifle granted by the gunsmith class ability, but they gain specialized powers for use after they gain weapons of advanced technology. A techslinger has the following class features.
::ab[Deeds]{head icon=info note="Techslingers have access to all gunslinger deeds, except as noted below for new deeds that replace standard gunslinger deeds."}

::ab[Covet Charge (Ex)]{next l=1 icon=boost ability="A techslinger can spend 1 grit point to use 1 charge fewer than normal when firing a ‹main/tech«nological» weapon<s› (minimum 0), as long as the weapon has enough charges remaining to be fired at least once." replace="‹deed/Deadeye›"}
::ab[Reliable (Ex)]{next l=1 icon=def free="A techslinger can spend 1 grit point to prevent a *timeworn* firearm from glitching." replace="‹deed/Quick clear›"}

::ab[Charge Recycling (Ex)]{next l=11 icon=power ability="By spending 1 grit point, a techslinger can grant 1 temporary charge to a technological firearm, even if the firearm normally can no longer be recharged. This charge must be used within 1 hour or it fades. The techslinger can grant temporary charges to multiple firearms as long as she has enough grit, but temporary charges do not stack with themselves in the same firearm." imp15="The techslinger can grant 2 temporary charges when she uses this deed." imp19="She can grant 3 temporary charges." replace="‹deed/Expert loading›"}
::ab[Heavy Weaponry Deeds (Ex)]{l=11 icon=power passive="As long as the techslinger has at least 1 grit point, she can treat a *heavy weapon* as a firearm for the purpose of using deeds."}
::ab[Bonus Feats]{icon=power ability="A techslinger can select ‹feat/Technologist› as a bonus feat, even though it's neither a combat nor a grit feat."}

::ab[Technic Training (Ex)]{icon=boost-def l=5 order=cp choice="A techslinger can select one specific type of advanced technology firearm (such as a ‹tech-weapon/laser pistol›, ‹tech-weapon/mindrender›, ‹tech-weapon/stun gun›, or ‹tech-weapon/zero rifle›)." passive="She gains a bonus equal to her Dexterity modifier on damage rolls when using that firearm. Furthermore, she treats all detrimental ‹rule/weapon_>glitches› of *timeworn* versions of her selected firearm as a result of no glitch. (She can retain the effects of beneficial glitches if she wishes.)" imp9="The techslinger selects another type of advanced technology firearm, gaining these bonuses for that type as well." imp13="She selects a third type, gaining the same benefits as above." imp17="She selects a fourth type, gainaing the same benefits as above." replace="Gun training"}

thronewarden.name: Thronewarden
description: ## Thronewarden
‹SOURCE Heroes of the High Court/8›

As vigilant sentinels, thronewardens identify and head off trouble. First employed in the Grand Duchy of Alkenstar, they are now also found in the jungle courts of the Mwangi Expanse, the lesser chambers of Nex's Council of Three and Nine, and the Hurricane King's gatherings in the Shackles.
::cskill{gain="Sense Motive" lose=Bluff}

::ab[Hair-Trigger Reflexes (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power-boost order=ap ability="As long as she has at least 1 grit point, a thronewarden can act in a surprise round even if she doesn't notice her enemies, though she remains ‹rule/flat-footed› until she acts." passive="She gains a +1 bonus on initiative checks." increment="The initiative bonus~2~4" imp10="She can spend 1 grit point to take both a @HLstandard_action and a @HLmove_action (or a @HLfull-round_action) during the surprise round." replace=Nimble}

::ab[Eye for Trouble (Ex)]{l=4 icon=boost ability="A thronewarden can spend 1 grit point when she attempts a Perception or Sense Motive check to roll 1d6 and add the result to the check. She can do this after she attempts the check but only before the result is revealed." replace="The bonus feat gained at 4th level"}
::ab[Deeds]{head icon=info note="A thronewarden has access to the following deeds."}

::ab[Warning Shot (Ex)]{next l=7 icon=power-lower standard="A thronewarden with at least 1 grit point can purposely miss a creature that she could normally target with a firearm attack and make it second-guess its actions. The creature is ‹misc/staggered› on its next turn. If a thronewarden uses this ability in a surprise round on a creature that has not yet acted, that creature instead loses its surprise-round action if it has one." replace="‹deed/Startling shot› deed"}

::ab[Opening Shot (Ex)]{next l=11 icon=power standard="A thronewarden can call her allies to action in a surprise round by spending 1 grit point and shooting a firearm into the air. Any allies within 30 feet who would not normally be able to act in the surprise round can act at the end of the surprise round (their order is determined by their initiative results; they act in normal initiative order in following rounds). Allies who would normally act in the surprise round but who have not yet acted can take a full round's worth of actions instead of just a move or standard action." replace="‹deed/Bleeding wound› deed"}

wyrm_sniper.name: Wyrm Sniper
description: ## Wyrm Sniper
‹SOURCE Dragonslayer's Handbook/23›
These long-shot artists are masters at taking down dragons with sharp shooting and light siege weaponry.
The following are class features for wyrm snipers.
::cskill{gain="Knowledge (arcana)"}
::ab[Artillery Proficiency]{icon=power ability="Wyrm snipers are proficient with light ‹rule/siege engines›, in addition to the normal gunslinger weapon proficiencies."}
::ab[Deeds]{head icon=info note="A wyrm sniper swaps a trio of deeds for the following deeds."}

::ab[Munitions Master]{next l=7 icon=boost ability="A wyrm sniper can spend 1 grit point to reduce the number of full-round actions (or move actions, if the sniper has the Master Siege Engineer feat) required to load a light siege engine by 1." replace="‹deed/Startling shot› deed"}

::ab[Skeleton Crew]{next l=11 icon=boost ability="As long as a wyrm sniper has at least 1 grit point, she treats all light siege engines as requiring a crew of one fewer than normal to operate (minimum 1)." replace="‹deed/Lightning reload› deed"}

::ab[Anti-Air Targeting]{next l=15 icon=lower ability="Whenever a wyrm sniper targets a creature's wings with the ‹deed/targeting› deed, the wyrm sniper completely disables one of the creature's wings on a successful hit. If the wyrm sniper hits her target, the creature falls to the ground, regardless of its altitude, taking the appropriate amount of falling damage as a result. As long as the target has at least one functioning wing, it may attempt a DC 20 Fly check to gain the benefits of ‹spell/feather fall› instead of plummeting to the ground. In either case, it must succeed at a Reflex save (DC 10 + 1/2 the wyrm sniper's level + the wyrm sniper's Wisdom modifier) or fall ‹rule/prone› when reaching the ground." replace="‹deed/Menacing shot› deed"}
::ab[Siege Commander (Ex)]{l=4 icon=power ability="A wyrm sniper may select the ‹feat/Siege Commander› combat feat as her bonus feat, even if she does not meet the prerequisites."}

::ab[Heavy Gunner (Ex)]{icon=boost l5="A wyrm sniper may select one specific type of light siege weapon (such as a ‹eq-siegeengine/ballista<_light› or ‹eq-siegeengine/light bombard›) in place of a specific type of firearm as part of her *gun training* class ability." l9="The gunslinger may again select another type of light siege engine." l13="She may again select another type of light siege engine." l17="She may again select another type of light siege engine." alter="Gun training"}

::ab[Master Siege Engineer (Ex)]{l=12 icon=power ability="A wyrm sniper may select the ‹feat/Master Siege Engineer› combat feat as her bonus feat, even if she does not meet the feat's prerequisites."}
